title: Sequential Search

content.problemStatement:
Sequential or Linear search is a method to search for an element in an array. 

The algorithm for sequential is very simple and it works on unordered (unsorted) data as well.

To search for an element in an array, initialise an index to the start of the array and iterate it forward till you either reach end of the array or found the element your are looking for.

TaskComplete the implementation of the sequential search algorithm to solve this problem. 

Return the index of the element (1-based) if it exists in the array, else return -1.
constraints: Array elements are distinct and fit in 32‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and X, where N is the size of the array and X is the target element. The second line contains N distinct integers, the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the 1‑based index of X in the array if it exists; otherwise output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case the target 1 is at position 2 (1‑based). In the second test case the target 6 does not appear, so -1 is printed.
The target 3 is found at the third position.

solution.approach: We simply scan the array from left to right keeping a 1‑based position counter. As soon as we encounter the target X we record its position and stop scanning (or continue, the first occurrence is enough because elements are distinct). If the scan finishes without finding X we output -1. This is the classic sequential (linear) search which works for unsorted data and runs in O(N) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input array

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int N=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int X=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int ans=-1;for(int i=1;i<=N;i++){int val=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());if(val==X && ans==-1){ans=i;}}sb.append(ans).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
